Should the Board of Directors be authorized to borrow from or utilize non-structural reserve funds in the event that the Association incurs a cash flow shortage in the 2026 operating budget due to delinquent payments or other unexpected operating needs? The borrowed monies must be returned to the reserve account by the end of the 2026 fiscal year. WAIVING OF RESERVES, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, OR ALLOWING ALTERNATIVE USES OF EXISTING RESERVES MAY RESULT IN UNIT OWNER LIABILITY FOR PAYMENT OF UNANTICIPATED SPECIAL ASSESSMENTS REGARDING THOSE ITEMS. (Board recommends voting Yes on this)

Should the Board of Directors be authorized to borrow from or utilize non-structural reserve funds in the event that the Association incurs a cash flow shortage in the 2026 operating budget due to delinquent payments or other unexpected operating needs? The borrowed monies must be returned to the reserve account by the end of the 2026 fiscal year. (Board recommends voting Yes on this)
Board of Directors (West or East Tower)
Amendment #1:  Define the Board of Trustees as 5 Trustees, 2 from the West building, 2 from the East building.  (This reinstates the rules which were removed from the 2023 Amendments.)

Amendments #2 and #4: Require Trustees to hold an election to fill a Trustee vacancy, rather than appointing Trustees to fill a vacancy, as has been done for the last several years; extend the time period to set up an election from 14 days to 21 days.
Amendment #3:  Require quarterly Owner meetings with financial and management reports, increasing from one required meeting per year to 4.
Amendment #5:  Reduce quorum requirement from 50%+ of beneficial interest to 35%+ beneficial interest.
Amendment #6: Require monthly publication of Trustee's votes, saying how each Trustee voted on each issue that was brought to a vote.
Amendment #7: Confirm authority to use e-voting for elections and ballots (clarifies 2023 Amendment).
